# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Pull Request Build Notification
on: [pull_request]
jobs: 
    notify-prs:  
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest     # Use the latest version of Ubuntu or any other Linux distribution. Change as needed, also set up .github/workflows to enable this workflow at repository level on GitHub UI (Settings -> Actions)
        
        steps: 
          - name: Get PR info       # Name for step which runs before action   
            uses: actions/pull-request-event@v2                  
            
          
          - name: Add commit msg   # Step to add the pr title as a comment in each pull request. Change if not required (You can use 'echo ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }} > description' instead) 
            run : echo "$(github.event.pull_request.title)" >> $GITHUB_STREAM_FILE     # Use it to add the PR title into a file in your workflow for later use (Change as needed).   You can also replace 'echo'.    The script adds an existing pull request event data variable's value with echo command, here added pr body.
        
          - name: Echo Pull Request description # Step to add the PR Description into notification system    
            run : |                                            # Using pipe (|) character will allow running multiple lines at once using 'run'.    You can also use more commands in this step based on your requirements, here are a few examples.  echo ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }} >> description  >> $GITHUB_STREAM_FILE
        
          - name: Show pull request details # Step to print out the event data variables' value    
            run : |                                                                    # Using pipe (|) character will allow running multiple lines at once using 'run'.  Here are a few examples. echo ${{ github.event.pull_request.* }} >> description  >> $GITHUB_STREAM_FILE      Printing all pull request event data variables' value in Yaml file
